question: How long do you retain our data? What is your data deletion process? How do you prove deletion?
title: Data Retention Policies, Deletion Mechanisms, and Proof of Erasure
bullets: Define retention periods by data type (raw data, logs, backups, analytics, metadata)
Describe deletion workflow (soft delete, hard delete, cryptographic erasure, key destruction)
Explain how deletion propagates across all systems (production, caches, backups, logs, partners)
Provide verifiable proof of deletion (audit logs, certificates, attestations)
suggested_inputs: Data retention policy documentation by data category
Deletion workflow diagrams across system components
Sample deletion certificates or audit logs
Backup retention and overwrite policy documentation

good.text: We define retention by data type: raw inputs (30 days), logs (90 days), backups (30–60 days). Deletion includes immediate soft delete, followed by hard delete and cryptographic erasure via key destruction. Policies are documented and configurable.

Deletion propagates across all systems: production databases, caches, logs, analytics pipelines, and backups. We track deletion status to ensure no residual data remains beyond defined retention windows.

We provide proof of deletion through immutable audit logs, deletion certificates, and optional third-party attestations. Cryptographic erasure ensures data cannot be reconstructed once keys are destroyed.
Customers can configure retention periods and request immediate deletion. Our processes are aligned with GDPR Article 17 (Right to Erasure), including handling of backups and derived data.

partial.text: We retain data for a defined period, but retention is not clearly broken down by data type or system.
We delete data upon request, but details on how deletion propagates across backups and logs are limited.
We maintain internal logs for deletion, but do not provide formal certificates or third-party verification.
Backups are retained for operational purposes, but selective deletion or overwrite timelines are not clearly defined.

bad.text: We follow standard retention policies without specifying durations or data categories.
Data is deleted when the customer cancels, without explaining how or providing proof.
We retain anonymized data indefinitely for product improvement without clarifying re-identification risks.
Backups are retained for several years and cannot be selectively deleted.

follow_ups: If we delete data today, explain how deletion propagates across production systems, caches, backups, logs, and partner systems.
Provide a sample deletion certificate or audit log proving data erasure.
How do you handle deletion in immutable backup systems where selective removal is not possible?
What metadata or derived data persists after deletion (e.g., logs, analytics)?
Can you guarantee compliance with GDPR Article 17 (Right to Erasure) across all systems?
